title: Linear Algebra
description: Understand vectors, matrices, transformations, eigenvalues, and their applications in computer science.

title: Probability and Statistics
description: Apply probability theory, distributions, and statistical inference to model uncertainty in computational systems.

title: Graph Theory
description: Represent and analyse graphs, implement traversal algorithms (BFS, DFS), and solve shortest-path and spanning-tree problems.

title: Lambda Calculus
description: Understand the foundations of functional computation, alpha-equivalence, beta-reduction, and Church encodings.

title: Logic and Proof Techniques
description: Construct formal proofs using propositional and predicate logic, induction, and proof by contradiction.

title: Compiler Design
description: Implement lexers, parsers, semantic analysers, and code generators; understand intermediate representations and optimisation passes.

title: Memory Management
description: Manage heap and stack allocation, understand garbage collection strategies, and reason about ownership and borrowing.

title: Design Patterns
description: Apply creational, structural, and behavioural design patterns to build maintainable and extensible software systems.

title: API Design
description: Design RESTful and GraphQL APIs; apply versioning, pagination, and error-handling conventions.

title: Distributed Systems
description: Reason about consistency, availability, and partition tolerance; implement consensus protocols and distributed data stores.
